message.content:
# tar (npm) — Security Knowledge Base Entry

---

## 1. Audit History

| Date | Reviewer | Scope | Key Finding | Outcome |
|------|----------|-------|-------------|---------|
| 2025-07-13 | KB Automation Pass | OSV advisory review, npm registry check, SECURITY.md check | 15 published CVEs spanning 2015–2026; all fixed in latest (7.5.13). No SECURITY.md found. | Entry drafted; no unpatched issues at current version. |
| 2025-07-13 | KB Automation Pass | Version/download posture | Latest npm version 7.5.13; ~65.4M weekly downloads. | Confirmed high-criticality ecosystem dependency. |
| 2025-07-13 | KB Automation Pass | Advisory chain analysis | Three distinct "eras" of symlink/hardlink path traversal fixes identified (2018, 2021, 2026). | Pattern documented; recommendations issued. |
| 2025-07-13 | KB Automation Pass | Comparison triage (ws, handlebars) | tar selected as highest-signal entry for this pass based on advisory density and ecosystem reach. | tar entry prioritized. |

---

## 2. Known Vulnerabilities

Advisories are grouped by era and attack class. All listed CVEs are fixed in the current release (7.5.13).

| Era / Chain | CVEs (representative) | Attack Class | Severity Context | Fixed In (earliest fix branch) | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **Legacy (2015–2018)** | CVE-2015-8860, CVE-2018-20834 | Symlink & arbitrary file overwrite | High — full file overwrite on extraction | 2.0.0 / 2.2.2 / 4.4.2 | Foundational extraction safety issues in early major versions. |
| **2021 Symlink/Cache Poisoning Chain** | CVE-2021-32803, CVE-2021-32804, CVE-2021-37701, CVE-2021-37712, CVE-2021-37713 | Insufficient symlink protection, directory cache poisoning, absolute-path sanitization bypass, Windows relative-path flaw | High to Critical — multiple sequential bypasses of path safety | 3.2.2–3.2.3 / 4.4.14–4.4.18 / 5.0.6–5.0.10 / 6.1.1–6.1.9 | Five CVEs in rapid succession (Jul–Aug 2021). Indicates the fix surface was complex; each patch addressed a variant or bypass of prior fixes. |
| **2024 DoS** | CVE-2024-28863 | Denial of Service via folder-count parsing | Moderate — resource exhaustion on crafted input | 6.2.1 | Single issue; distinct class from path traversal chain. |
| **2025–2026 Memory & Path Traversal Chain** | CVE-2025-64118, CVE-2026-23745, CVE-2026-23950, CVE-2026-24842, CVE-2026-26960, CVE-2026-29786, CVE-2026-31802 | Uninitialized memory exposure, symlink/hardlink path traversal (incl. drive-relative linkpath, Unicode ligature collisions on macOS APFS), symlink chain escape | High to Critical — seven CVEs across v7.x; multiple bypasses of hardlink/symlink protections | 7.5.2–7.5.11 | Pattern closely mirrors the 2021 chain: sequential fix-then-bypass cycle in the same extraction path safety logic. Includes novel attack surfaces (APFS Unicode normalization, drive-relative paths). |

---

## 3. Security Posture Notes

- **Recurring fix-bypass pattern.** Both the 2021 and 2025–2026 advisory chains show a characteristic pattern where an initial path-traversal fix is followed by multiple bypass CVEs in rapid succession, suggesting the underlying sanitization model is difficult to make exhaustive in a single pass.

- **No SECURITY.md or documented vulnerability disclosure policy** was found at the repository root (`https://github.com/isaacs/node-tar`). Reporting channels are not publicly surfaced beyond GitHub's default advisory mechanism.

- **Extremely high blast radius.** At ~65.4 million weekly npm downloads, tar is a transitive dependency of npm itself and a vast portion of the Node.js ecosystem. Any extraction-time vulnerability has broad supply-chain impact.

- **Active maintenance demonstrated.** Despite the advisory volume, fixes have been shipped promptly across supported major-version branches, and the maintainer has engaged constructively with each report. The 7.x branch received seven security patches within a short window in 2025–2026.

- **Attack surface is inherently complex.** Tar extraction must handle symlinks, hardlinks, absolute paths, drive-relative paths, platform-specific filesystem semantics (APFS Unicode normalization, Windows drive letters), and
